items.title: Amazon is betting on speed in a market that may not need it
items.summary: Quick commerce promises instant convenience, but it’s driven more by deep discounts and habit-building than real need.

items.title: Nations priced out of Big AI are building with frugal models
items.summary: Amid a widening global divide in AI adoption, low-cost AI models that can deliver sovereignty and efficiency with a smaller environmental footprint are gaining ground.

items.title: In the Gulf, GPS jamming leaves delivery drivers navigating blind
items.summary: The war has led to mass GPS-jamming,  forcing drivers to rely on memory, landmarks, and phone calls.

items.title: “This is unprecedented”: America’s AI boom is leaving the rest of the world behind
items.summary: Tech leaders called AI a democratizing force. But it’s concentrating power and wealth in a handful of American companies.

items.title: Workers around the world are not getting what they want from AI
items.summary: A survey of workers in 60 countries found that a majority of those facing the threat of AI-driven job loss do not trust companies or governments to manage the transition fairly.

items.title: The world’s largest humanoid robot maker is going public
items.summary: The Unitree robot is pictured at the Web Summit at Parque das Nacoes in Lisbon, on November 11, 2025. The Web summit, the annual gathering of the digital economy and new technologies, will take place from November 10 to 14. (Photo by PATRICIA DE MELO MOREIRA / AFP) (Photo by PATRICIA DE MELO MOREIRA/AFP via Getty Images)

items.title: EVs were meant to bypass oil. Now they’re stuck at the Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: The war has blocked the only sea route for the high-grade, low-carbon aluminum EVs need. There's no quick substitute.

items.title: AI glasses are catching on in China, from shopping to cheating
items.summary: Early adopters are renting AI glasses for $6 a day for navigation, translation, and school exams.

items.title: Missiles slow Dubai’s tech scene — but don’t shake it
items.summary: Workers in the financial district of Dubai on Wednesday, March 4, 2026.

items.title: Meta, YouTube verdict can ripple through social media markets worldwide
items.summary: PARIS, FRANCE - JUNE 15: The logo of the U.S. company created by Mark Zuckerberg, Meta is displayed during the Viva Technology conference at Parc des Expositions Porte de Versailles  on June 15, 2023 in Paris, France. Viva Technology, the biggest tech show in Europe but also in a unique digital format, for 4 days of reconnection and relaunch thanks to innovation. The event brings together startups, CEOs, investors, tech leaders and all of the digital transformation players who are shaping the future of the Internet. The annual technology conference, also known as VivaTech, was founded in 2016 by Publicis Groupe and Groupe Les Echos and is dedicated to promoting innovation and startups. (Photo by Chesnot/Getty Images)

items.title: Fire risks and ugly designs are stalling EV charger adoption
items.summary: From Seoul to New York City, residents are fighting charging infrastructure over safety, aesthetics, and crowding.

items.title: From Chile to the Philippines, meet the people pushing back on AI
items.summary: Individuals and communities are resisting the demands and practices of Big Tech's AI infrastructure — such as data centers and digital labor — due to their environmental and social costs.
